title: Productivity Jumps Stair-Step Infographic
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Intelligent Textbooks / AI-Assisted Content Creation
description: An interactive stair-step infographic showing how each new AI tool reduces the person-hours needed to build an intelligent textbook: Manual (3,000 hrs), ChatGPT (1,000 hrs), Claude Code (100 hrs), and Claude Code Skills (10 hrs). Click a step to reveal a detailed description of that tool.

learningObjectives: Illustrate how each generation of AI tooling reduces the effort needed to author an intelligent textbook
Compare the person-hours required across four authoring approaches on a log productivity scale
Explain why reusable Claude Code Skills produce an order-of-magnitude productivity jump over earlier tools
prerequisites: Familiarity with the idea of authoring digital educational content

controls.label: Step bars
controls.purpose: Click any step in the staircase to display its detailed description

controls.label: Prev / Next
controls.purpose: Move the selection one step down or up the staircase

controls.label: Reset
controls.purpose: Return the selection to the Manual baseline step

keyQuestions: How many person-hours does each authoring approach require?
Which transition produces the largest single productivity jump?
Why does encoding workflows as Skills outperform prompting individual pages?
commonMisconceptions: Productivity gains from AI tooling are incremental rather than order-of-magnitude
All AI assistance offers the same speed-up regardless of how it is applied
assessmentOpportunities: Ask students to rank the four approaches by person-hours and justify the ordering
Have students estimate the speed-up multiplier between adjacent steps
